final_prompt.prompt:
Create an original, non-infringing meticulous gongbi heavy-color handscroll painting depicting the Lantern Festival panorama in Tang Dynasty Chang'an city.

Style/medium: gongbi (工笔) heavy-color handscroll (长卷), Tang Dynasty court painting aesthetic
Composition: wide horizontal panorama scroll format, sweeping cityscape view across the full width, layered depth from foreground crowds to distant city gates and towers
Subject: tens of thousands of hanging and floating lanterns (万盏花灯) illuminating the night sky above Chang'an; festival crowds in Tang Dynasty attire filling the streets and plazas; ornate lantern towers, silk-draped pavilions, and imperial boulevard
Scene/backdrop: Tang Dynasty Chang'an city at night during the Lantern Festival (元宵灯会); grand city walls, vermilion gate towers, wide ceremonial avenues lined with lantern trees
Lighting/mood: warm golden glow from myriad lanterns casting rich amber and crimson light across the night scene; festive, luminous, celebratory atmosphere
Color palette: rich vermilion, imperial gold, deep indigo night sky, jade green, mineral azurite and malachite pigments consistent with Tang gongbi heavy-color tradition
Materials/textures: fine-line brushwork with layered mineral pigment washes on silk scroll; visible silk texture ground, meticulous detail on lantern patterns and costume embroidery
Text (verbatim): 
Constraints: horizontal long-scroll (长卷) format; gongbi line precision throughout; night scene must be lit primarily by lantern light, not moonlight or torches
Avoid: ink wash (水墨) loose style, Western perspective, modern lighting effects, Song or Ming dynasty architectural styles
